expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Case focus is explicitly covered: context intake preserves banding requirements, style constraints, and rendering assumptions before scenario drafting
expectations.evidence: result.md explicitly frames the benchmark intent as Phase 1 context intake preserving “Banding requirements”, “Style constraints”, and “Rendering assumptions” before scenario drafting, and lists the specific requirements from the Jira payload (rows/columns/header-based banding, color formatting limits, Modern Grid in dashboards). It also states this should be captured during Phase 1 spawn-manifest planning (pre-scenarios).

expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Output aligns with primary phase phase1
expectations.evidence: Both execution_notes.md and result.md scope the work to Phase 1: “Output intentionally scoped to Phase 1 … and does not draft scenarios” and “Phase 1 contract alignment … Phase 1 output is phase1_spawn_manifest.json,” focusing on intake/manifest planning and explicitly noting absence of runtime artifacts rather than producing later-phase scenario drafts.
